rulings.Case Title: EICON INC VS WORLDPOINT INTERACTIVE INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The Basis Of Waiver And Equitable Estoppel Re: X-Complainants' Causes Of Action For Equitable Indemnity And Related Declaratory Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 18, 2010, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT MASSIMO FUCHS Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The Basis Of Waiver And Equitable Estoppel Re: Cross-Complainants' Causes Of Action For Equitable Indemnity And Related Declaratory Relief. Motion denied. Equitable indemnity arises when a party has been compelled to pay damages which a wrongdoer should have paid. (See Herrero v. Atkinson (1964) 227 Cal.App. 2nd 69, 74). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS JOSEPH BACHLEDER VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Hearing On Deft'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 18, 2010, line 7. DEFENDANTS MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH INCORPORATED'S (Amended) Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay Action. Motion granted. Arbitration clause is contained in the 2006 U4 form that requires plaintiff to arbitrate dispute with his "firm". B A I was succeeded by Merrill Lynch which assumed all rights and liabilities. Arbitration clauses are also contained in the Bonus agreement and promissory note. No showing made that any arbitration clause in unconscionable, invalid, or unenforceable. All other matters stayed pending completion of arbitration. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LETHA N SMALL VS. THERESA LUCHINI et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 18, 2010, line 8. PLAINTIFF LETHA SMALL'S Motion For Default Judgment is off calendar. To obtain a default judgment, plaintiff must obtain it from the clerk or through a prove up hearing. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 18, 2010, line 12. PETITIONER WESLEY RUSCH'S Motion To Strike. Motion to strike denied as untimely under CCP section 1005. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WESLEY RUSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Petition Wesley Rusch Pursuant To Ccp Section 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 18, 2010, line 12. RESPONDENT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S Motion For Sanctions Against Petition Wesley Rusch Pursuant To CCP Section 128.7. Motion for sanctions denied. Plaintiff never filed the second motion for reconsideration with the court and therefore CCP section 128.7 does not apply and there is nothing to withdraw. See Trans-Action Comm't Inverstors, Ltd. v. Telinek (1997) 60 Cal.App. 4th 352, 369. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE DYMPNA SWIFT VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner'S Order To Show Cause To Respondents (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-11-10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 18, 2010, line 13. Petitioner's Order To Show Cause To Respondents. Petition for writ is granted. Respondents must fully comply with their statutory duties as specified in W & I Code section 18914 and 7 U.S.C. section 2020. Respondents must screen all expedited service applications on a priority basis at the time an applicant initially seeks assistance (W & I Code section 18914(b)) and comply with the deadlines regarding the receipt of expedited benefits. Writ denied as to oral notification requirements of W & I Code section 18912 as respondents have fully complied. Respondents must file a return to this writ on or before June 30, 2010 setting forth what they have done to comply. =(302/CWW)
